what_it_does: This noticeboard item records a design argument and a three-part implementation plan for treating the dragnet's existing field-index triple-store as a traversable graph by wiring code:// addresses and selected relation types into the traversal engine.
observations: The dragnet currently produces three graph-like outputs: a flat field-index triple-store, embedded extraction:// objects with k-NN traversal, and a board scanner (cc_board).
The field-index has 11.5k rows and lives in ops/code_archaeology.py.
query_field_index(field, value) is framed as structurally equivalent to reverse_traverse(subject=?, predicate=field, object=value).

The proposed fix is called 'the one wire' and is split into three small pieces: A (code:// resolution in resolve_address), B (register declares/imports/symbol relation types), and C (wire reverse_traverse to read the field-index).
Piece A alone is described as testable and enough to let board items link to code.
The file uses internal address schemes such as code://, extraction://, and board://.
standouts: The central claim that the field-index is not a missing abstraction but a missing connection to the traversal engine.
The 'one wire' plan with explicit, small line-count estimates (keystone ~20 lines, C ~40 lines).
Making code:// nodes first-class addressable entities as the keystone change.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter (id, address, type, source, state, title, links, timestamps).
Uses markdown headings and bullet lists for structured argumentation.
References internal URI-like address schemes (code://, extraction://, board://).
Includes bold emphasis for key terms and claims.
concerns: The line-count estimates are speculative and not grounded in the file.
The plan assumes resolve_address already has a board:// branch and a place to add code:// handling, but those internals are not shown.
It does not define the shape or validation rules for the declares/imports/symbol relation types.
No code symbols or implementation snippets are present to verify the proposed wiring.
notes: A concise design pitch that the dragnet's flat code field-index is already a triple-store and only needs a small traversal-engine wire to become useful.
questions: Where is resolve_address implemented and what does the existing board:// branch look like?
What is the exact schema and storage format of the field-index in ops/code_archaeology.py?
How does reverse_traverse currently work, and what would reading the field-index require?
What are the semantics of the declares/imports/symbol relation types and how are they registered?
What does 'k-NN traversable' mean for extraction:// objects and the corpus op?
What tests or acceptance criteria are intended for piece A?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a design note that explains the dragnet-as-graph idea and proposes a minimal 'one wire' implementation path.
apparent_themes: Graph isomorphism between query interfaces and traversal operations
First-class code nodes via address resolution
intention_signals: It is trying to persuade the team that a small, well-scoped wiring change can unlock graph traversal over existing code facts, starting with making code:// addresses resolvable.
novelty: It reframes a flat, unused field-index as a complete triple-store that lacks only a traversal connection, and packages the fix as a single 'one wire' with three small, ordered parts.
contribution: The conceptual mapping between query_field_index and reverse_traverse, plus a concrete three-step wiring plan (code:// resolution, relation-type registration, reverse_traverse integration) that a fused system could use to connect code facts to the traversal engine.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item item-068c8b19 is a design note proposing to wire the dragnet's existing typed field-index triple-store into the traversal engine by adding code:// address resolution, registering declares/imports/symbol relation types, and connecting reverse_traverse to the field-index.
intention_for_embedding: To argue that the dragnet already contains a traversable code graph and that only a minimal three-part connection is needed to make code nodes first-class and traversable.
